channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All MOUSE: PI For Hire Voice Actors—Who Voices Jack Pepper?
item.content:encoded:
Trying to be a voice actor in the gaming industry is a tough gig with so many talented workers out there, and Fumi Games managed to put together one heck of a line-up when assembling voice actors for MOUSE: PI For Hire.

One of the things that stood out to me while playing MOUSE: PI For Hire was the voice acting—it’s richer than a multi-layered cheesecake. An incredible cast of characters is brought to life by an even more talented roster of voice actors and actresses. It’s time to honor them and give them their flowers.

Oh, and if it’s somehow eluded you, spare some time and read my in-depth MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Review!

Who is Jack Pepper’s Voice Actor in MOUSE: PI For Hire?

MOUSE: PI For Hire’s Jack Pepper is played and voiced by none other than Troy Baker.

It wouldn’t be far-fetched to say that Troy Baker is the Brad Pitt of the video game voice acting world. Troy is known throughout the medium for his roles as Joel from The Last of Us, Sam from Uncharted 4, and Booker DeWitt from Bioshock Infinite, among COUNTLESS others.

Baker’s incredible range seems like a perfect fit for Jack Pepper.

  • More Insider Gaming: MOUSE: PI For Hire Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

Jack is the star of the show, and Baker’s delivery makes every pun even punnier, giving Jack a pronounced edge, cementing the character’s story as a war veteran.

I would be remiss if I didn’t applaud a certain dialogue exchange in the game’s middle if you find and locate a certain stinky fish!

All Voice Actors in MOUSE: PI For Hire

Troy Baker, Florian Clare, Camryn Grimes, Fred Tatasciore, and Frank Todaro are recognized as the ‘main’ characters in MOUSE: PI For Hire.

Unfortunately, we don’t have time to go through everyone who’s contributed to this stellar game, so here’s a quick rundown of the voice actors given the spotlight and a little about them.

Troy Baker as Jack Pepper

According to IMDB, Baker’s acting credits now exceed 400 roles. He’s primarily known for gaming, and while he was a presence in the game’s industry for years, it really was his role as Joel Miller in The Last of Us that shot him to superstardom. It was also a nice touch to have him appear in The Last of Us TV Show.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete MOUSE: PI For Hire Trophy List

Jack Pepper is one of many main acting credits for Baker, who’s also had huge roles in Death Stranding 2, Indiana Jones and the Golden Circle, God of War Ragnarok, and seemingly every game released in the last five to ten years.

Florian Clare as Wanda Fuller

Wanda Fuller is a consistent presence throughout MOUSE PI, and she’s played wonderfully by Florian Clare. The actress has played parts in Silo, Industry, and even some video game roles, such as Antandra in AFK Journey.

Camryn Grimes as Tammy Tumbler

Are you ready for an incredible fact about Camryn Grimes? According to her IMDB page, she has appeared in 1,318 episodes of The Young and the Restless! In between juggling other TV roles, unless something is missing from the resume, MOUSE: PI For Hire marks Grimes’ video game debut.

Fred Tatasciore as John Brown

We definitely don’t have time to go through Tatasciore’s portfolio, as he has over 1,000 voice acting credits across video games, TV, and movies—including the box office sensation The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

Frank Todaro as Cornelius Stilton

Finally, the jolly politician Cornelius Stilton is played by Frank Todaro—a man known for impeccable facial hair in real life. Todaro has many credited roles, including Code Vein 2, Borderlands 4, Yakuza 0: Director’s Cut, and Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth.

Do you agree that the voice acting is one of the reasons MOUSE PI is so enjoyable? Would you love to see Troy Baker reprise his role as Jack Pepper in a sequel? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more guides, check out if MOUSE: PI For Hire is on Xbox Game Pass and if it has a Physical Edition with a disc.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: The Witcher’s Zoltan Voice Actor Has Passed Away
item.content:encoded:
Some unfortunate news has arrived for fans of The Witcher video games as Zoltan’s voice actor has passed away.

A new post on the official The Witcher account on X (formerly Twitter) reveals that Alexander Morton, the voice actor for Zoltan Chivay, has passed away. He portrayed the beloved character in both The Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings and The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt. The cause of his passing wasn’t revealed.

Zoltan’s Voice Actor Had Several Games Under His Belt Apart From The Witcher Series

The Zoltan voice actor, Alexander Morton, was 81 at the time of his passing and has lent his voice to various games beyond The Witcher. The first video game he worked on was Heavenly Sword, which was followed by Viking: Battle for Asgard, Age of Conan: Hyborian Series, Fable 2, Killzone 2, Ni No Kuni: Wrath of the White Witch, and LittleBigPlanet for PS Vita. Apart from that, he has worked on several TV movies and series.

Talking about Zoltan, he was a beloved character of the Witcher series and a friend of Geralt. He is also a veteran of the Second Nilfgard War and met Geralt when going towards the Yaruga River from Brokilon. Zoltan is also the man who provided Geralt with Sihil. The character hasn’t died in the games and is a key ally in the main books; therefore, it is unclear how CD Projekt Red will address this situation.

In other news, The Witcher 4 team has expanded to 499 developers at CD Projekt Red. Additionally, a new mod has arrived for The Witcher 3, which lets you get a glimpse of The Witcher 4 early. What are your thoughts about The Witcher’s Zoltan actor passing away? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox’s rumored first-party Game Pass tier receiving monthly time allowances for cloud gaming. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: T1 LCK Woes Continue Despite Keria’s Best Efforts
item.content:encoded:
T1 threw away their lead against Dplus Kia (DK) and lost yet again as the League of Legends titans continue to struggle in the LCK.

The reigning LoL World Champions, who’ve won the last three iterations of the international tournament, find themselves barely in the Playoff places, tied for sixth with a 2-3 record.

T1 being inconsistent during the regular season is nothing new, but the last few performances have some fans starting to worry.

T1 Lose 2-1 To DK

In the opening match, Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun tried to repeatedly gank top to help Choi ‘Doran’ Hyeon-joon but failed multiple times. In one case, DK’s Jeon ‘Siwoo’ Si-woo survived with just 24 health.

Meanwhile, T1 botlaner Kim ‘Peyz’ Su-hwan kept getting dived alone under his turret as DK racked up a healthy lead.

From there, DK executed well and snowballed their advantage in Dragon Soul to take the lead in the best-of-three.

But T1 then tied up the series after winning across all three lanes in a much better performance. Though they almost let the game slip through their fingers.

It looked like T1 was about to throw the game at the end during a sketchy Dragon fight.

However, a perfectly timed Zhonyas by Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok distracted three enemies at once and allowed Oner to clean up and secure the ace for the win.

Faker Mistakes Undo Keria’s Hard Work

Then, in the decider, T1’s bot lane earned first blood for the second match in a row with Peyz boldly flashing forward on his Mel after hitting level 2.

That unlocked the rest of the map for Ryu ‘Keria’ Min-seok’s Pyke as he then successfully ganked both mid and top before the five-minute mark.

Keria then ganked top another three times in the early game, finding takedowns every time as T1 surmounted a significant early lead.

One particularly impressive play saw Keria steal a minion to hit level six, then immediately flash and ult to secure another kill.

However, Faker got caught twice in quick succession, and DK started to find their way back into the game.

Pyke is a champion that quickly falls off and becomes much trickier to pilot, which allowed DK to win most of the team fights.

Keria was playing out of his mind and made yet another incredible play to win a skirmish for T1, yet his team still couldn’t get a foothold on the game.

Faker got picked off a couple of more times, which allowed DK both Dragon and Baron to tie up the gold.

At this point, Keria’s Pyke was getting deleted as soon as he engaged, which left T1 without any tools to win fights.

This allowed DK to complete a full comeback and win the series in what could be a crucial result for them.

Do you think T1 will recover and make it to MSI? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And reminisce about peak T1 as the champion choices for the T1 LoL Worlds 2025 Championship skins have been confirmed.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Xbox’s Rumored First Party Game Pass Tier Could Cap Your Cloud Gaming Allowance
item.content:encoded:
New backend data reveals that Xbox is testing monthly time allowances for Cloud Gaming on the rumored first-party Game Pass tier.

For those unaware, a report from last month stated that a new Game Pass tier, codename TRITON, is being tested and will only include first-party games from Xbox Studios, including franchises like Doom, Halo, Fallout, and more. Now, the latest findings discuss some restrictions for Cloud Gaming included in this tier.

Xbox Could Get Monthly Time Allowances for Select Cloud Gaming Tiers

According to a recent post by redphx on X (formerly Twitter), WindowsCentral has reported that Xbox’s rumored first-party Game Pass tier, TRITON, along with another speculated tier called Duet, which is expected to be a bundle with Netflix, will include support for Xbox Cloud Gaming. However, there is a caveat: Cloud Gaming on these tiers will have certain restrictions, and players will need to follow a monthly time limit. This means Cloud Gaming will stop working once players reach a designated monthly limit of hours.

It’s not uncommon for Cloud Gaming services to feature monthly limits, with Nvidia GeForce Now’s highest limit offering only 100 hours of playtime per month. However, it is new for Xbox, as none of their tiers have ever had monthly allowances for cloud gaming.

Regarding Cloud Gaming, Xbox reportedly plans to launch an ad-supported tier in 2026. This model will also include time limits, where players can enjoy a specific number of hours of gameplay based on the number of ads they have watched. Furthermore, players might only be able to access Xbox games they have purchased digitally, with no access to Game Pass titles.

In other news, the runtime of the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day presentation has been revealed by the Xbox COO. Additionally, a claim states that Xbox is having internal discussions about its games and the concept of first-party exclusives. What are your thoughts on Xbox’s rumored first-party Game Pass tier introducing monthly time allowances for Cloud Gaming? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Gear of War: E-Day giving Cliff Bleszinski ‘Goosebumps’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Is Saros Releasing On PC? All Saros Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Saros is one of the biggest games releasing in 2026 and has rightfully built up a lot of attention. A Returnal successor was always going to grab headlines, but Saros’ platforms and PC status have left you all more than a bit confused.

Sony started something big a few years ago when it decided to publish PlayStation-exclusive games on PC. It’s allowed God of War, Ghost of Tsushima, Horizon, and more to flourish with an additional platform, more sales, and additional exposure. Housemarque’s Returnal was also given this special treatment, but in Saros, it’s not so simple.

Can You Play Saros on PC?

You can’t play Saros when it launches day one on PlayStation 5, and based on reports, it doesn’t seem like PC players will be able to play Saros at all.

Well, you CAN own a PS5 and PC, I should clarify, so you can technically play Saros if your main format is PC, but don’t expect a Saros PC port like other recent PlayStation exclusives.

  • More Insider Gaming: Saros Review Embargo Date Countdown

It’s hard to stay afloat of all gaming news as it constantly refreshes and moves at a pace. But Insider Gaming reported on March 4 that Sony is no longer releasing single-player PlayStation Exclusives on PC.

This means Saros, Wolverine, and many others will remain PS5-exclusive releases. Sony still hasn’t confirmed this just yet, so there’s still a modicum of hope. The likelihood is that if you do have a tanky PC and you’re looking forward to Saros, you might have to stick with Returnal as the closest alternative.

Can You Play Saros on Xbox?

Saros will not be coming to Xbox Series X|S or Xbox One consoles at any point.

Whereas PC players have been fortunate to cross some boundaries with Sony, Xbox has had no luck. The relationship has been the other way around, if anything. Gears of War, Forza, and Halo have all been released or are being released on PS5, but it’s a one-way street.

Can You Play Saros on Nintendo Switch?

You knew the answer, but let’s cross all bridges by saying Saros isn’t coming to either the Nitnenod Switch or Switch 2.

The Switch 2 is a beast. It’s getting more big-time third-party releases than its predecessor, but Sony and Nintendo aren’t in the business of swapping exclusives with each other. So, you won’t see Pickahu mixing it up with Nathan Drake, and you won’t find Arjun stepping on the toes of the Mushroom Kingdom.

  • More Insider Gaming: Saros Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Now’s the time to head on over to the Insider Gaming Discord Server to let our audience know what you think of Saros. Will it be yet another success for Sony and Housemarque?

For more on the sequel, read about Saros’ PS5 Pro enhanced features.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Gear of War: E-Day Giving Cliff Bleszinski ‘Goosebumps’
item.content:encoded:
Cliff Bleszinski hasn’t had much involvement with the video game industry in recent years, but the legendary Gears of War dev has admitted that what he’s seen from Gears of War: E-Day is giving him ‘goosebumps’ and has high hopes for the game.

As Lead Designer on the first three Gears of War games, Cliff Bleszinski etched himself into the upper echelons of game development folklore. The original trilogy is a triumph, and while he did not influence the games thereafter, Bleszinski thinks the upcoming prequel is ‘writing the ship.’

Cliff Bleszinski ‘Can’t Wait’ For Gears of War: E-Day

The reception to Gears of War: E-Day, so far, has been incredibly positive. It looks like a proper return to form, reverting back to some of the original characters that made the earlier games so memorable and beloved.

Not that there’s been anything wrong with Gears 5 or Gears of War 4—far from it!

But Gears of War: E-Day looks solid, and when quizzed about the game on The Expansion Pass (through Idle Sloth), Bleszinski said this:

“I think they’re doing the whole go back to give the people what they want. I think the possibilities for cinematic moments are tremendous with that they’re doing. I got goosebumps when I saw, Marcus falling in that hand, went down and it end up being Dom. I think, they’re really, writing the ship. You’re not going to be fighting those stupid robots or anything, it’s just going to be making the Locust scary again, and seeing Marcus, you know, struggle with just one Locust in that trailer, I think was brilliant. And I have high hopes for it and I can’t wait to see what they put out for it.

If I like it, I’m going to be honest about it, and if I don’t like it, I got nothing to lose, to be honest.”

We learned recently from a key Gears of War voice actor that The Coalition nearly made Gears 6 instead of E-Day.

This would have been cool, and it’s potentially going to be the next big project anyway, but E-Day will be a welcome return to a bygone Gears era.

Is a return to Gears of War’s roots long overdue? What are you hoping for in the Gears of War: E-Day story? Share your thoughts with the whole team and our IG community through the Insider Gaming Discord.

For a big look at the upcoming prequel, check out the Gears of War: E-Day Direct runtime being revealed by the Xbox CCO.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…
